flags.concern: Step 4 would create a preview/editor mismatch: clamping only in the Remotion render layer changes the media box that the user sees, but the overlay editor still derives handles and hitboxes from the raw clip metadata. Until Step 5 lands, the visible image and the draggable box will disagree.
flags.evidence: Render styles come from `getClipBoxStyle()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:16-58`. Overlay bounds are computed independently from raw `clipMeta` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:152-186` and surfaced in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:218-256`.

flags.concern: The core fix assumes a browser/Remotion max-element limit is the active failure mode, but this repository's preview path is DOM-based, not canvas-based. A hard 8192px cap is not justified by the code here and may not address a preview bug at all.
flags.evidence: The preview uses `@remotion/player` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx:95-128`, and `VisualClip` renders images/videos as DOM `` / `

flags.concern: The plan does not account for the existing full-bounds vs visible-bounds crop model. Persisting a clamped width/height back into clip state would discard the original full size, so restoring the original dimensions when the asset comes back into view becomes impossible and crop math can drift.
flags.evidence: `OverlayEditor` explicitly separates `fullBounds` from cropped visible bounds via `getVisibleBoundsFromCrop()` and `getFullBoundsFromVisibleBounds()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:74-99`, and pushes full-bounds data back through `onOverlayChange()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:417-429`.

flags.concern: The overview is factually off about repository behavior: `ClipPanel` already constrains width/height inputs to the composition dimensions. Step 6 would not add the first UI guardrail; it would loosen the current cap from composition size to `MAX_ELEMENT_DIMENSION`, which is a product-behavior change the plan does not justify.
flags.evidence: The current position inputs already set `max={compositionWidth}` and `max={compositionHeight}` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:225-231`.
